details: The Mukhya Mantri Seekho Kamao Yojana, implemented by the Technical Education and Manpower Planning Department, aims to provide On-the-Job Training (OJT) to youth with formal education in registered industrial and commercial establishments. This initiative encourages industries and businesses to actively participate in skill development while enabling young individuals to gain practical experience. ₹1,00,000/- youth will benefit annually, with the flexibility to increase the target based on demand. Additionally, each participant will receive a stipend of up to ₹1,00,000/-, funded by the state government, during their training period.
benefits: Skill Enhancement: Offers practical training to improve job readiness.
Stipend Support: Provides up to ₹1 lakh as financial aid during training.
Graduate or Higher Qualification: ₹10,000 per month.
eligibility: The applicant should be between 18 to 29 years of age.
The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant should be educated with a minimum qualification of 12th pass, ITI, or higher.
The applicant should be designated as a “Student-Trainee” upon selection.

faqs.question: What is the Mukhya Mantri Seekho Kamao Yojana?
faqs.answer: It provides on-the-job training and stipends to youth in Madhya Pradesh.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Youth aged 18-29, local residents of Madhya Pradesh, with 12th/ITI or higher qualifications.

faqs.question: What stipend will be provided?
faqs.answer: 12th pass: ₹8,000, ITI pass: ₹8,500, Diploma pass: ₹9,000, Graduate or higher: ₹10,000.

faqs.question: How can I register?
faqs.answer: Visit the official website, fill in the details, upload documents, and submit the application.

faqs.question: What documents are required?
faqs.answer: ID proof and relevant certificates are required during registration.

faqs.question: Can I apply for the scheme if I am pursuing my studies?
faqs.answer: No, only youth who have completed their 12th, ITI, or higher education can apply.

faqs.question: Is the stipend fixed for all participants?
faqs.answer: The stipend varies based on the educational qualification of the applicant.

faqs.question: Can I change my training center during the program?
faqs.answer: Changes to the training center are subject to approval and availability.

faqs.question: What happens after completing the training?
faqs.answer: After completing the training, candidates may be offered job opportunities depending on their performance.

faqs.question: Is the scheme available in all districts of Madhya Pradesh?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme is available statewide, and applicants from all districts can apply.
